title: Context Room project configuration
description: Configuration for the local Context Room documentation control room.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URL for editor autocomplete.

projectOnly.description: When true, ordinary project paths must remain physically inside this project root after symbolic links are resolved. Fresh setup writes true. False, or omission in a legacy config, can make configured external symlink targets readable and editable; retain that mode only for trusted established hubs.

allowedPaths.description: Safety boundary. Context Room can only read/write editable text files inside these project-relative files or folders and explicitly listed ~/... home paths.

readOnlyPaths.description: Allowed project-relative or explicit ~/... paths that Context Room may display but must never create, edit, or delete. Shared accepted snapshots should be exposed here.

watchAllow.description: Simple files or folders to monitor for the review queue. Folder entries use recursive-live behavior: current and future files at any depth can appear for verification.

watchRules.description: Explicit folder watch rules covered by allowedPaths. Use these when a folder needs direct-child scope or a frozen current-file snapshot instead of the default recursive-live behavior.

reviewPaths.description: Ordered important files or folders that stay in the review queue until a human verifies the current content, even when there is no Git diff. Critical safety issues still appear first.

reviewAgentInstructions.description: Whether project AGENTS.md files are implicit required-review paths. Disable this only for a room whose human review scope is intentionally narrower.

sharedContext.description: Generated shared-context connection summary. Use `context-room shared setup` instead of editing this object directly.

markdownTemplates.description: Markdown templates available when creating new .md files from the site. Supports {{title}}, {{path}}, and metadata placeholders such as {{scope_yaml}}, {{status_yaml}}, {{canonical_for_yaml}}, {{last_verified_yaml}}, {{sources_inline}}, and {{sources_list}}.

startupContext.description: Optional scanner for agent instruction files in the project or, when projectOnly is false, its ancestors and explicit global paths. External files stay separate from the normal explorer.

projectOnly.description: When true, scan only this project root and ignore ancestor and explicit global instruction files.

globalPaths.description: Explicit global agent instruction files outside the ancestor chain, such as ~/.codex/AGENTS.md.

startupSkills.description: Optional scanner for project skill folders or, when projectOnly is false, matching folders in ancestor directories.

projectOnly.description: When true, scan skill folders only inside this project root instead of every ancestor directory.

startupHooks.description: Optional scanner for hooks and hook-manager files that can affect agent work, commits, or validation.

projectOnly.description: When true, scan agent hook configuration only inside this project root. Effective Git hooks and project hook-manager files remain project-scoped.

editable.description: When true, Context Room can edit detected hook files. Off by default because hooks execute code.

agentHooks.description: Scan AI coding agent hook configuration and plugin files, including Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and compatible tools.

agentHookSources.description: Configurable AI coding systems to show in Startup hooks. Each source has a label and one or more project-relative config/plugin paths.

id.description: Stable source id used for filtering.

label.description: Human-readable agent system name, such as Codex or My Agent.

paths.description: Config files or plugin folders to scan for this agent system.

codexHooks.description: Legacy alias for agentHooks. Kept for existing Context Room configs.

gitHooks.description: Scan effective Git hook directories, including .git/hooks and core.hooksPath.

hookManagers.description: Scan common hook-manager files and folders such as Husky, Lefthook, pre-commit, lint-staged, and package.json hook config.

codexPaths.description: Legacy alias paths for older configs. Prefer agentHookPaths.

hubCards.description: Legacy card visibility map. Prefer hubSections for new setups.

customHubCards.description: Legacy flat custom card list. Prefer hubSections for new setups.

hubSections.description: Top-level groups shown in the Context Room hub.

integrations.description: Optional integrations. Keep false/empty for generic projects.

hermes.description: Enable Hermes-specific files and external skill paths. Off by default for generic projects.

path.description: Allowed folder governed by this rule, conventionally written with a trailing slash. It can be project-relative or an explicit ~/... path already listed in allowedPaths.

mode.description: recursive-live watches current and future files at any depth; recursive-current snapshots current files at any depth; direct-current snapshots current immediate file children; direct-live watches current and future immediate file children.

files.description: Frozen eligible file paths for a current-file mode. Paths are normally project-relative; configured ~/ integrations are also supported. Context Room populates this list when the rule is created.

autoChildren.description: When true and the card points to a folder, Context Room infers child cards from the folder's immediate files and subfolders.

enabled.description: When false, keep this template editable in settings but hide it from the apply-template selector.

content.description: Template body. Supports {{title}}, {{path}}, and metadata placeholders such as {{scope_yaml}}, {{status_yaml}}, {{canonical_for_yaml}}, {{last_verified_yaml}}, {{sources_inline}}, and {{sources_list}}.
